bark_725c20f65b6d39b9.text: Do you think those guys were triad?
bark_725c20f65b6d39b9.speaker: Carter

bark_e9f19588a2a710ea.text: Not sure. I don't recognize their tattoos.
bark_e9f19588a2a710ea.speaker: Duncan

bark_15f86a93fb1ffe33.text: That sniper means business! Stay in cover!
bark_15f86a93fb1ffe33.speaker: Duncan

bark_cdddf88a0b06af5d.text: Step back, or you'll regret it.
bark_cdddf88a0b06af5d.speaker: Yellow Lotus Guard

bark_2d89f3d31ceccd03.text: We should't be here when they come running.
bark_2d89f3d31ceccd03.speaker: Unknown

bark_2a60cc7e53afe1e6.text: Guns at the ready! We're going up.
bark_2a60cc7e53afe1e6.speaker: Security Officer

bark_0897400967386741.text: Wait! It was just part of the drill!
bark_0897400967386741.speaker: Wuxing Employee

bark_78115a590c509d3f.text: Get in there and secure the body!
bark_78115a590c509d3f.speaker: Security Officer

bark_bd452c22d5791272.text: Light and dark balance is key to maintain good Qi.
bark_bd452c22d5791272.speaker: Unknown

bark_ade40288949efff4.text: Well, they look happy to see us...
bark_ade40288949efff4.speaker: Unknown

bark_e0dfd02880ab38e1.text: Why is it so cold in here?
bark_e0dfd02880ab38e1.speaker: Unknown

bark_fba649cd7a2231ef.text: I'm sure you'll get the part. Your audition was excellent!
bark_fba649cd7a2231ef.speaker: New Actor

bark_c3568952df156263.text: That's wonderful! When will you get a call back?
bark_c3568952df156263.speaker: New Actor

bark_5e0160061acdb066.text: Did you hear he's half-Russian? It's no wonder he gets those overseas jobs.
bark_5e0160061acdb066.speaker: New Actor

bark_17e17add819d2498.text: I'm gonna have to make it for three million less than my original budget.
bark_17e17add819d2498.speaker: New Actor

bark_5e47cf2be0bd8af1.text: That party looks like it's really fancy. Who's throwing it?
bark_5e47cf2be0bd8af1.speaker: New Actor

bark_7fe578a947ab8b72.text: I don't know, but I could swear I saw Penelope Wong in there.
bark_7fe578a947ab8b72.speaker: New Actor

bark_b2d0ed677401eef2.text: Oh man. I'd love to meet her. She's so glamorous!
bark_b2d0ed677401eef2.speaker: New Actor

bark_391b8f09e4009c0f.text: Yeah, ten orders! I'm having a party!
bark_391b8f09e4009c0f.speaker: New Civilian

bark_f64b55a145e9d5b9.text: The boss is in the back. Go on through.
bark_f64b55a145e9d5b9.speaker: Triad Blue Lantern

bark_5b74b4ccf39621ea.text: Activate a drone to send into the vent.
bark_5b74b4ccf39621ea.speaker: Unknown

bark_c595cdacdcc57e7c.text: I've picked my teeth with tougher bastards than you!
bark_c595cdacdcc57e7c.speaker: Unknown

bark_d8d50e7580c85f8d.text: Guns are no match for my fists!
bark_d8d50e7580c85f8d.speaker: Unknown

bark_16b6fd4a9b185204.text: Devil rats! Don't let them bite you!
bark_16b6fd4a9b185204.speaker: Unknown

bark_86c7f8a510fe6e02.text: Come join us by the fire and get warm.
bark_86c7f8a510fe6e02.speaker: $(scene.Global_Name_CompelledLocal2)

bark_660043ff54f08280.text: We don't want to do this, but we don't have a choice!
bark_660043ff54f08280.speaker: $(scene.Global_Name_CompelledLocal2)

bark_ef078e6e9d58b70f.text: It's just over here. Follow me.
bark_ef078e6e9d58b70f.speaker: $(scene.Global_Name_CompelledLocal1)

bark_6667471dc0969741.text: Hey, Malvina! I've got something of yours.
bark_6667471dc0969741.speaker: Gobbet

bark_8e00640e7094d7c5.text: There's the Shiny Object! She must be feeding on the thing!
bark_8e00640e7094d7c5.speaker: Gobbet

bark_af32d6d4a1a0394e.text: What the hell did I tell you?! Waste this idiot!
bark_af32d6d4a1a0394e.speaker: Red Spear Ganger

bark_7c3a94457d1fa5d0.text: That's got it, this way to the suites!
bark_7c3a94457d1fa5d0.speaker: Unknown

bark_39de327e8362016b.text: ...Of course, this year's model will feature our new "Lightningwire" jack interface technology!
bark_39de327e8362016b.speaker: $(scene.Convention_ConGoer3Name)

bark_955219f9feceb4e4.text: Does that mean that it won't be compatible with older jack interfaces?
bark_955219f9feceb4e4.speaker: $(scene.Convention_ConGoer3Name)

bark_1ca460214af610d5.text: Unfortunately, we couldn't bring you the ultimate in datajack uplink speeds without redesigning the interface.
bark_1ca460214af610d5.speaker: $(scene.Convention_ConGoer3Name)

bark_7a41fb130f045ace.text: You *will* have to update your datajack to take advantage of these exciting new features. But I have these vouchers to help you with that...
bark_7a41fb130f045ace.speaker: $(scene.Convention_ConGoer3Name)

bark_3da9380ed81a2b3c.text: Excuse me! A presentation is underway, please walk around.
bark_3da9380ed81a2b3c.speaker: $(scene.Convention_ConGoer3Name)

bark_9effc39d1ad14b2e.text: The food here is always drek. Why do we keep buying it?
bark_9effc39d1ad14b2e.speaker: $(scene.Convention_ConGoer3Name)

bark_20b969707530501c.text: Because eating this is better than waiting in line to get back into the Con.
bark_20b969707530501c.speaker: $(scene.Convention_ConGoer3Name)

bark_97d110131dad5e13.text: Let's go, clear out of the server room.
bark_97d110131dad5e13.speaker: Hotel Security Guard

bark_46b30e6ddb70de37.text: Hey! I said no entry. I need back up on the bottom floor!
bark_46b30e6ddb70de37.speaker: Convention Security Guard

bark_2119b4b8b9d48ee9.text: Hello?! Security! I had to flee to room 203. Quick get me out of here.
bark_2119b4b8b9d48ee9.speaker: Rhombus

bark_0db0acdf9ddef6e1.text: We're in position and we've secured the target.
bark_0db0acdf9ddef6e1.speaker: HKPF Captain

bark_ceaed999ffdd6931.text: I thought we were through the worst of it.
bark_ceaed999ffdd6931.speaker: Unknown

bark_654245a80dc28440.text: Chatty customers always killing my tips.
bark_654245a80dc28440.speaker: Waitress

bark_de5df95a8e40013e.text: That crab was truly amazing. I just wish I didn't have to wait an hour to get it.
bark_de5df95a8e40013e.speaker: New Civilian

bark_10ebbb97db411af2.text: I said there are intruders aboard!
bark_10ebbb97db411af2.speaker: Unknown

bark_0e4bd8765b46a4cd.text: Raise the alarm, we're under attack!
bark_0e4bd8765b46a4cd.speaker: Unknown

bark_798ec61ec2f7dcff.text: Stop them before they raise the alarm!
bark_798ec61ec2f7dcff.speaker: Unknown

bark_7b4d31b2ef3210bc.text: There's an emergency on C Deck!
bark_7b4d31b2ef3210bc.speaker: Security Enforcer

bark_7f3ed02865435bbe.text: Here they are! I've found the intruders!
bark_7f3ed02865435bbe.speaker: Unknown

bark_9200e18861a6c731.text: We've got intruders aboard! Call for backup!
bark_9200e18861a6c731.speaker: Unknown

bark_051550d0201a6a3b.text: I think that's the last of them...
bark_051550d0201a6a3b.speaker: Unknown

bark_be9f4f9273968890.text: [In Cantonese] Give it now, or you die!
bark_be9f4f9273968890.speaker: $(scene.OuterRing_RedPoleTriad_Name)

bark_675151473653319b.text: So much for honor among theives!
bark_675151473653319b.speaker: $(scene.OuterRing_LocalShaman_Name)

bark_0537f5c5c2962189.text: No. NO! Not the teeth. SO many teeth.
bark_0537f5c5c2962189.speaker: Kowloon Local

bark_db8f35056c8eef28.text: S--so many. They just. Claws. Wouldn't stop.
bark_db8f35056c8eef28.speaker: Kowloon Local

bark_2ddbf101ecd19ba3.text: Can't. NO! WON'T take me. I refuse.
bark_2ddbf101ecd19ba3.speaker: Kowloon Local

bark_22e21d7690db167a.text: Never. They listen; and now they are gone.
bark_22e21d7690db167a.speaker: Kowloon Local

bark_214acaed551fd7cf.text: Stop! Just stop, you can have it!
bark_214acaed551fd7cf.speaker: $(scene.OuterRing_LocalShaman_Name)

bark_ec38928a7d7f78bc.text: Got to be some way to get this door open.
bark_ec38928a7d7f78bc.speaker: Unknown

bark_e940aafbc217ecac.text: We are going to have company!
bark_e940aafbc217ecac.speaker: Unknown

bark_6324000b8830c19c.text: Got them here! Send back up!
bark_6324000b8830c19c.speaker: Tsang Enforcer

bark_8c62b486da2e05da.text: You gots the uniforms on now, placeholder.
bark_8c62b486da2e05da.speaker: Unknown

bark_15ba5a3bf2793640.text: Just drop it off in the back room and clear out.
bark_15ba5a3bf2793640.speaker: Tsang Captain

bark_d45450774109f502.text: Just wait a few minutes and try not break anything!
bark_d45450774109f502.speaker: Tsang Captain

bark_638cad19f8f23403.text: Tell the second squad to fall back!
bark_638cad19f8f23403.speaker: Unknown

bark_19f7eb86120b39b8.text: But I don't see a mahjong parlor.
bark_19f7eb86120b39b8.speaker: Duncan

bark_75e9b4905f0d2045.text: Come in. We have a lot to talk about.
bark_75e9b4905f0d2045.speaker: Kindly Cheng

bark_957eefc1c277dbdd.text: (We can hide in the MTR tunnels!)
bark_957eefc1c277dbdd.speaker: New Actor

bark_c655d125bae84a91.text: I can't believe this. Trapped in my own apartment building!
bark_c655d125bae84a91.speaker: Resident

bark_127c5fcd760a58fa.text: They won't even tell us why the building's on lockdown!
bark_127c5fcd760a58fa.speaker: Resident

bark_f8e1d30f8f457b33.text: Thanks, come by in person I have something for you.
bark_f8e1d30f8f457b33.speaker: Beaten Suspect

bark_9386f6df32e36c5e.text: Look, we will be here unitil Krait says otherwise!
bark_9386f6df32e36c5e.speaker: SDU Combat Mage

bark_e46edc8540cd823b.text: ...scorpyrine? I thought that was your wife!
bark_e46edc8540cd823b.speaker: Knight Errant Guard

bark_94bd8becc765bc0a.text: Hong Kong - Victoria Harbour. August, 2056. Monsoon Season.
bark_94bd8becc765bc0a.speaker: Narrator

bark_7165181612e89703.text: Signalling to the Employee, he heads for the checkpoint.
bark_7165181612e89703.speaker: Unknown

bark_e35aebc104f1785a.text: The qi is becoming more and more turbulent. It has reached a point where this churning energy has a physical manifestation visible in the atrium. The disruptions are working.
bark_e35aebc104f1785a.speaker: Narrator

bark_88b42f2ebe64ebd9.text: The Repulse Bay Mezzanine, Hong Kong Island.
bark_88b42f2ebe64ebd9.speaker: Narrator

bark_307f0749b18d90e7.text: As you sift through the pile, you hear a rustling sound. Two beady eyes lock with yours.
bark_307f0749b18d90e7.speaker: Unknown

bark_56e9b0929a6044e1.text: You hit the ground with a bone-snapping crunch. Everything goes black.
bark_56e9b0929a6044e1.speaker: Narrator

bark_c2cd921d1b4db030.text: An ominous chittering noise fills the air.
bark_c2cd921d1b4db030.speaker: Narrator

bark_3c6524db60a1b76f.text: You hear the scrape of claws on metal. Something is heading this way.
bark_3c6524db60a1b76f.speaker: Narrator

bark_b03a8723a21f4ad4.text: Energy courses through Gobbet's body. She flexes and the Rat King's servitors move in response.
bark_b03a8723a21f4ad4.speaker: Unknown

bark_6716fd205b3b9fd7.text: The lights in the Engine Room  flicker. All across the raft, shipboard systems begin to fluctuate and fail.
bark_6716fd205b3b9fd7.speaker: Narrator

bark_4974a12a764951bf.text: A thunking sound accompanies the arming of the scuttling charges. They're live and ready to blow.
bark_4974a12a764951bf.speaker: Narrator

bark_397540ec022c6cdb.text: The Rat King recoils, but only for a moment. A strange energy washes over it, bathing its body in indigo light.
bark_397540ec022c6cdb.speaker: Narrator

bark_f2971f63868c2b5a.text: You better find someplace indoors to sleep, Yan. The streets are dangerous lately.
bark_f2971f63868c2b5a.speaker: Unknown

bark_c9a7d8c112597644.text: The Elders don't want anyone to go inside without their permission. It must be pretty bad in there.
bark_c9a7d8c112597644.speaker: Unknown

bark_6662c91c7d22aaae.text: I don't know. I just don't feel safe here any more!
bark_6662c91c7d22aaae.speaker: Unknown

bark_e9bf538ccda510f0.text: I heard he's not just dead, he's been all cut up...
bark_e9bf538ccda510f0.speaker: Unknown

bark_5a8e6e82a49c0eed.text: You lift the decker's credstick with ease.
bark_5a8e6e82a49c0eed.speaker: Narrator

bark_3ec1fafe49f024e1.text: You use Rooster's service key copy to open the door.
bark_3ec1fafe49f024e1.speaker: Narrator

bark_ce8bf74e52c1844b.text: The docking door clangs and slides open. It's time to get to the escape boat.
bark_ce8bf74e52c1844b.speaker: Narrator

bark_1c7b593f60102caf.text: I can't believe they are late again.
bark_1c7b593f60102caf.speaker: Unknown

bark_120ca8de9009d189.text: Say again? I didn't copy that.
bark_120ca8de9009d189.speaker: Unknown

bark_9c8c140a2a68f918.text: Please repeat, we didn't copy that.
bark_9c8c140a2a68f918.speaker: Unknown

bark_e2038c45b1abbaf4.text: Say again, patrol 3? We couldn't hear you.
bark_e2038c45b1abbaf4.speaker: Unknown

bark_2cf95b76b23d814f.text: The commotion from the streets of Heoi bleeds into Kindly Cheng's mahjong parlor. Triad soldiers bustle to keep up with their boss's orders. Couriers come in regularly, whispering to her lieutenant before listening intently and running off again.
bark_2cf95b76b23d814f.speaker: Narrator

bark_1a00ae8f47c64b79.text: Fear grips the people streaming from inside the Walled City. The storm isn't a typhoon. It's something else.
bark_1a00ae8f47c64b79.speaker: Narrator

bark_2240732e9011ae8a.text: Select a Decker to Jack In.
bark_2240732e9011ae8a.speaker: Unknown

bark_caaf095ae6ec16c1.text: HEOI. A makeshift boat-city latched onto the Kai Tak riverfront like a parasite. Behind you, a massive snarl of dark and derelict high-rises dominates the horizon. The Walled City.
bark_caaf095ae6ec16c1.speaker: Narrator

bark_a7d3f230fe61ca8b.text: The Swift Winds Mahjong Parlor - filled with the stink of cigarette smoke, the incessant click-clack of mahjong tiles, and the grim faces of hardened gamblers.
bark_a7d3f230fe61ca8b.speaker: Narrator

bark_738f2aa1870a3ec4.text: You hear a telecom begin to ring, near the cryotanks.
bark_738f2aa1870a3ec4.speaker: Unknown

bark_263a7181a1cdfda3.text: You disengage the locks on the hot lab safe.
bark_263a7181a1cdfda3.speaker: Unknown

bark_000b7278c87f8f53.text: Armory access panel set to maintenance mode.
bark_000b7278c87f8f53.speaker: Unknown

bark_a4e3924f2589a8ba.text: Medical access panel set to maintenance mode.
bark_a4e3924f2589a8ba.speaker: Unknown

bark_303c579459a1d079.text: Already accessible at panel with PDA.
bark_303c579459a1d079.speaker: Unknown

bark_c34f585d2814c5a4.text:
Raymond Black. The old man gave you a home once. Took you and Duncan off the gang-ridden streets of the Barrens - sheltered, educated, slapped sense into you both, until you almost resembled productive members of society.

And then you took off. Left it all behind. Landed behind bars for a time, tried to start a new life after that. It's been eight years since you've heard Raymond's voice. Until out of the blue, you got this cryptic message, a plea for help: "Meet me in Hong Kong, right away." And, wired to your account, enough nuyen to pay for the flight and then some.

The descent is rough. A squall comes out of nowhere, sending a solid sheet of rain punching into the suborbital transport. With a ragged shudder, the plane finally skids to a halt at the edge of the Chek Lap Kok tarmac. An hour and an interminable number of emotionless security checkpoints later, you hail a water taxi to Victoria Harbour. Hong Kong looms ahead, pulsing with energy.
bark_c34f585d2814c5a4.speaker: Narrator
bark_c34f585d2814c5a4.title: HARD LANDING

bark_a00cfcd06ab1d0b9.text:
Kowloon Walled City. The most densely populated spot on Earth - nearly 40,000 people crammed into seven acres of chaos, poverty, disease, and vice. A self-contained city that collects no taxes and provides no city services. Stagnant water sits in temporary wells. Trash lies piled on roofs for controlled burns. Improvised structures lean dangerously over populated areas.

It's the ideal breeding ground for all manner of illicit trade - drugs, gambling, black market trade, metahuman trafficking, and everything in between. The only law is triad law.

And now you need to enter this septic system of a city. Find a triad enforcer named Strangler Bao and deliver a message from Kindly Cheng.
bark_a00cfcd06ab1d0b9.speaker: Narrator
bark_a00cfcd06ab1d0b9.title: CITY OF DARKNESS

bark_9a7265d2f0a80414.text:
The subway flies over the tracks toward Aberdeen. Monsoon humidity clings to your skin, as if it's followed you from Heoi. Or perhaps it's simply sunk this far into the earth, penetrating stone and machine, rousing the acrid smells of Hong Kong's underground in its wake.

You've been handed a promising job - geomantic sabotage - with a single objective: disrupt the flow of Wuxing qi to destabilize the corporation. Your client is especially keen on the idea of Wuxing's famed geomancy being used against it. The ultimate humiliation.

While the corporation's office spaces are free game, the main target is Wuxing's treasured lotus statue, which resides within a temple on the topmost floor of the monolithic building. Your client's instructions are clear. The more damage you do, the worse the qi, and the better the pay.
bark_9a7265d2f0a80414.speaker: Narrator
bark_9a7265d2f0a80414.title: BAD QI

bark_046f4841e3777b3c.text:
You arrive at the eponymously named Repulse Bay - a gleaming hotel and apartment building on the shores of Repulse Bay, Hong Kong Island. Rolling stormclouds choke the sky, lending the structure a sinister appearance. As you push through the doors and into the building, a sudden break in the clouds reveals a sun that's gone red as blood.

You make your way into a ground floor elevator. The attendant pays you no mind. As the car begins to climb, you hear the sounds of merriment drawing closer.

As the doors slide open, you find Neville Ma's party in full swing.
bark_046f4841e3777b3c.speaker: Narrator
bark_046f4841e3777b3c.title: EXIT, STAGE LEFT

bark_c3b732115b5a21d6.text:
Is0bel has modified a taser to introduce Dreamland’s cortical inhibitor program into the Plastic-Faced Man’s neural network. That should stop his headware from completing a memory wipe and allow you to interrogate him for information about Raymond Black.

But first you’ll need to find him.

Kindly Cheng’s made contact with an underground information broker named Xiaozhi, who has managed to obtain the Plastic-Faced Man's complete itinerary. With it, you can choose the time and place to perform an extraction and find out what he knows. 

Xiaozhi works out of an abandoned night market in Shek Kip Mei - the Shing House Court.
bark_c3b732115b5a21d6.speaker: Narrator
bark_c3b732115b5a21d6.title: THE EXTRACTION

bark_a6a58a0d8707e446.text:
Central Hong Kong spills out along Victoria Harbour, all glittering chrome and neon flash against the night's darkness. The skyscrapers are a jagged nest of mirrored knives thrust into the sky, rending the clouds as they ascend into the heavens.

The Ares Asia Holdings complex squats menacingly over Chater Road -  a steel and concrete tumor intertwined with nearby buildings in a web of skybridges and shared parking structures. You make your way inside, and ascend to the fifth floor - a public foyer that connects to the restricted areas of the complex.

As the elevator opens, you arrive in cold, dimly lit reception area. Beyond it, your quarry awaits.
bark_a6a58a0d8707e446.speaker: Narrator
bark_a6a58a0d8707e446.title: MISDIRECTION

bark_6d24c3bc76608520.text:
The pungent smells of musty stone, grease, and the remnants of neurostun gas mix in your nostrils as you rappel down the elevator shaft. Whatever frustrated Knight-Errant security officers are in pursuit are too far away to be heard. All around you, there is only darkness and the occasional red-hued maintenance light.

As you reach the bottom and pry open the heavy elevator doors, light comes flooding into the shaft.

With any luck, you're only minutes away from the MTR station, and with it, freedom.
bark_6d24c3bc76608520.speaker: Narrator
bark_6d24c3bc76608520.title: MISDIRECTION

bark_c4a9fb018bbc9297.text:
The Tolo Harbour Industrial Complex is a sprawling maze of warehouses, autofabrication facilities, shipping container mazes, and corporate power. If something corporate-owned is shipped through Hong Kong, it invariably moves through these streets.

The Renraku shipping facility lies far enough away from the heart of Tolo Harbour that you won't have to worry about corporate security other than the on-site guards - and Renraku's extraterritoriality means that any fighting won't risk backup from rent-a-cops.
bark_c4a9fb018bbc9297.speaker: Narrator
bark_c4a9fb018bbc9297.title: RETRIBUTION

bark_3eb3f673227a4a26.text:
Hung Hom Bay. Captain Jomo's converted speedboat chops through the rough water like a cleaver through chicken bones, launching you skyward with every wave that it hits. Your destination: The Sinking Ship, Gobbet's former home.

The ork pirate laughs and opens the throttle. Wind and rain lash your face as the floating amalgamation of shipping containers grows larger. You can see that it's stacked at least three containers deep, an inelegant brick of corrugated weathering steel. An assortment of pontoons, buoys, and other floatation devices have been lashed to the base of the raft to prevent it from tipping.

You don't know what's happening here, or why Gobbet felt the need to go face it alone... but one way or another, you're about to find out.
bark_3eb3f673227a4a26.speaker: Narrator
bark_3eb3f673227a4a26.title: THE SINKING SHIP

bark_18aa930f9fd957c1.text:
Whampoa Garden: a carnival of chrome and neon, rife with every manner of technology and artifice one can imagine. The entire area has the feel of a night market, save that chips are sold in lieu of steamed buns, and vendors hawk the latest drones rather than folk art. Something stalks these streets, hiding just out of sight. It stalks the Whampoan Elders - the leaders of the band of tech-savvy squatters that have claimed this neighborhood as their own. 

The streets smell of ozone and fear, and those Whampoans you pass in the MTR station have a haunted, furtive look in their eye. While the killer has struck only at the Elders thusfar, it may only be a matter of time before ordinary residents are menaced as well.
bark_18aa930f9fd957c1.speaker: Narrator
bark_18aa930f9fd957c1.title: OUTSIDER

bark_a5a9a315bebdc88b.text:
A chartered bus carries you from Kai Tak to a mid-range hotel in Tsuen Wan. All around you, the happy chatter of your fellow passengers fills your ears. You hear tech-speak in a variety of languages and dialects; talk about new lines of drones and decks, arguments over innovations in datajack technology, and baseless speculation about the next season of Urban Brawl.

Your guest badge - a glossy slip of laminated paper emblazoned with a flashy logo - hangs on a lanyard around your neck. Your ticket into the convention hall, and the only disguise that you should need.

The bus drops you in front of the Harbour Spires hotel, and you promptly circle around back to find a service entrance. Is0bel should be waiting for you inside.
bark_a5a9a315bebdc88b.speaker: Narrator
bark_a5a9a315bebdc88b.title: DECKCON 2056

bark_ef22e8c7d7f253af.text:
This job is a golden opportunity, in more ways than one. A raid of the in-development museum, the Emperor's Tomb. 

The museum's claim to fame is its location. It rests on top of ancient catacombs, where an excavation is underway to convert them into the museum proper. Intel has it that the workers are dying. Not from on-site accidents, but from magical disturbances in the area. Despite the hazards, development continues.

Your client, Mr. Drake, contacted you to retrieve two tomes. Probably ancient. Magical. Priceless. But it won't be a smash in the park. You have to get to the tomes before they're catalogued, where they'll afterward be near impossible to access.

As you near the museum, its height and grandeur looms unsettlingly over you. Even unfinished, it's reached an imposing size. At least that means it has plenty of room for pricey antiques. The best part? Mr. Drake needs this run to look unprofessional. Shattered glass and looting is encouraged.
bark_ef22e8c7d7f253af.speaker: Narrator
bark_ef22e8c7d7f253af.title: THE DIG

bark_6f696ef421435c48.text:
A tangle of marine decorum, restless lights, and ambrosial scents mingle atop a floating quay to accent the Shangri-La - Aberdeen Harbour's premier dining location. With competitive views of the surrounding bay, the restaurant attracts a steady crowd of tourists and Wuxing personnel.

The primary moneyman for the 289s, Cheung-Sing "Rooster" Lo, is enjoying a rare meal outside of triad-held territory. Intel says he's set up in one of the restaurant's private dining rooms - but he's not alone. Rooster keeps a personal bodyguard, a notorious ork called "the Talon," in addition to his regular security detail. You've seen photos of the Talon's handiwork... bodies so battered it's hard to tell where one bruise ends and the next begins.

Your mission: locate Rooster, extract him from the Shangri-La, and deliver him to your client alive. Simple, fast, low profile. Of course, even the simplest of runs can go sideways...
bark_6f696ef421435c48.speaker: Narrator
bark_6f696ef421435c48.title: UNINVITED GUESTS

bark_cd86a58ba3e448ea.text:
The ride out to the Nalchi is nerve-wracking. Captain Jomo seems an expert pilot, but massive waves threaten to scuttle his small boat at every turn. Deep thunder rolls in the distance, and at the edge of your vision, lightning occasionally illuminates your target: the MV Nalchi.

As Jomo pulls your small craft directly up to the bow of the Nalchi, where you're least likely to be spotted. He throws a rope ladder up over the side, hustling you aboard before pulling away.

Time to get to work.
bark_cd86a58ba3e448ea.speaker: Narrator
bark_cd86a58ba3e448ea.title: WHISTLEBLOWER

bark_97e9678d30543b2d.text:
The typhoon pours down as the triad soldiers fan out in front of you, leading the way into Kowloon Walled City. Raymond shuffles along, alert and intent. Although exhausted by all that has gone on, he drives himself forward, and will not accept help from anyone. Wu watches the windows and rooftops, his rifle never leaving his shoulder. He occasionally risks a glance at his foster-father, concern, frustration, and anger playing over his face like a slideshow.

The Walled City is worse than in your first visit. Even with the rain, you can feel something in the air. It’s thicker now and it clings to your skin like invisible filth. The miserable noises of slum life are gone, replaced by something worse. The sounds of death. The sounds of fear.

As you round a corner, the group stops before heading in deeper.
bark_97e9678d30543b2d.speaker: Narrator
bark_97e9678d30543b2d.title: CITY OF DEATH

bark_70cdd0fa2cfb9e42.text:
The deafening rattle of machinegun fire fills the air behind you, and the metallic tang of blood cuts through the stench of garbage. You press forward, wading through block after block of insanity, chaos, and death. All around you, the Walled City is descending into a churning cauldron of depravity and violence.

The comm's of no use. You've tried several times, but there's still no word from the B-Team - no sign of Raymond, or of Kindly Cheng's triad soldiers. Finally, you reach the center of this hellhole: an industrial hatch with its door ajar.

This is the place where Raymond Black built his infernal machine. The threshold across which Qian Ya, the Queen With a Thousand Teeth, clawed her way into this world.
bark_70cdd0fa2cfb9e42.speaker: Narrator
bark_70cdd0fa2cfb9e42.title: THE FORTUNE ENGINE

bark_1ad14829e41a0891.text: The elevator codes acquired, you head into the enclosed space, watching the ceiling access panel closely as you make your way to Lab Twelve. Light elevator music contrasts sharply with the smell of sweat and cordite. As the lights on the elevator hit twelve, you cover the door and wait.
bark_1ad14829e41a0891.speaker: Narrator
bark_1ad14829e41a0891.title: ASIST

bark_f4d622f5bddc2e3a.text:
Something is wrong.

The last thing that you remember, it was the night of the Kowloon City riots. You and your team were running an errand for Kindly Cheng, recovering an object before the inevitable police response turned the city upside down. Everything was going well, you had the item in hand, and then... nothing. The world went black.

A sudden explosion of pain behind your eyes jolts you back to the present. A nightmarish succession of dream-images flicker through your consciousness like a strobe light: a man with a pockmarked face. Slim fingers playing over the surface of your brain, leaving a slug's trail of agony in their wake. A second figure whose face you couldn't see, digging with relish through the scraps of memory that your tormentor left behind.

It was a rough night. And now, as your eyes snap open and adjust to the light, you find yourself in a reality that is little more comforting...
bark_f4d622f5bddc2e3a.speaker: Narrator
bark_f4d622f5bddc2e3a.title: RUDE AWAKENING

bark_81ff52594d3fad7c.text:
The glittering expanse of Hong Kong stretches out beneath the helicopter as Jomo weaves his way between the city's myriad skyscrapers. Rain spatters the tiny craft's windshield as it slices its way through the calm skies above the Central District. Despite the late hour, the city streets below still glimmer with activity.

Soon enough, the black-mirrored walls of Ikon Tower stretch up before you. Jomo pulls the helicopter in for a landing, depositing you on the tower's roof. Somewhere beneath your feet, Lilian Lai awaits.
bark_81ff52594d3fad7c.speaker: Narrator
bark_81ff52594d3fad7c.title: DETENTION

bark_d13e39cde02795de.text:
You lead Lilian Lai out of Ikon Tower, taking care to ensure that the public street in front of you is clear of guards. Central Hong Kong is surprisingly quiet; there is very little foot traffic at this hour, and only the occasional delivery van rolls by on the street in front of you.

Time to make contact with the mercenaries working for Yamatetsu, and hopefully get clear of the Fuchi forces behind you.
bark_d13e39cde02795de.speaker: Narrator
bark_d13e39cde02795de.title: DETENTION

bark_cd75fa275e229742.text:
The trip to Lee's loaned minisub is uneventful, as is transit to the area near Hong Kong where Shiawase has built their research laboratory. The sub descends swiftly, and you are kept company by the hissing of the oxygen scrubbers - and the occasional ping of active sonar striking your hull. As you near the coordinates for the lab, Jomo begins switching off all but the most essential of systems.

After a few minutes, brighter light filters through the portholes as you ascend into a moon pool, and there is a dull thud as the facility's clamp closes around the sub to lift it free.
bark_cd75fa275e229742.speaker: Narrator
bark_cd75fa275e229742.title: NAMAZU

bark_61679126595016b8.text:
The Drunken Mistake screams over Kowloon Bay, cutting through grey weather and greyer waters. Around you, grim faces watch the skyline ahead as Jomo steers you toward Krait's headquarters: The Tigers' Den.

Qiu and Lam have assured you that if everything goes to plan, the run should be relatively low-risk. Ample experience tells you that plans often go astray, and that low-risk shadowruns rarely stay that way. But you also know that the reward for this run outweighs the risk. The incriminating data from the Den's evidence room should be more than enough to bury Krait, expose her client, and cement your position with Kindly Cheng forever.

Jomo cuts the engines, and the Drunken Mistake drifts toward the station's loading dock. It's go time.
bark_61679126595016b8.speaker: Narrator
bark_61679126595016b8.title: THE TIGERS' DEN

bark_e149e27c04a4a2b8.text:
You watch the Benteng drift slowly out of view as the Drunken Mistake bobs turbulently on dark, choppy waves. Angry rain dimples the water's surface and obscures the world around you in a haze. Before long, the warehouses appear on the horizon - great grey silhouettes looming just beyond the docks.

Krait and her people are guarding a warehouse here. You don't know what you're going to find inside, but this is the last lead that you have to follow. If the conspiracy within the SDU is going to be stopped, it has to happen tonight, in that warehouse.

Qiu sits nearby, her fingers pressing into her temples - a sign of her distress, no doubt. But despite her evident discomfort, she still looks present and alert.

Tai Po - once a small fishing village, now encrusted with cheap industrial warehouses - grows ever closer. Water whips down from the sky as you bump up against the docks. You've arrived.
bark_e149e27c04a4a2b8.speaker: Narrator
bark_e149e27c04a4a2b8.title: TAI PO
